cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out how many penguins are more than 5 years old and weigh more than 12 kg. The data is given in a table, so I should probably go through each penguin one by one and check their age and weight. First, let me recall the data. The headers are name, age, height (cm), weight (kg). Then there are four penguins: Louis, Bernard, Vincent, and Gwen. Let me list each penguin's details: - Louis: age 7, height 50 cm, weight 11 kg - Bernard: age 5, height 80 cm, weight 13 kg - Vincent: age 9, height 60 cm, weight 11 kg - Gwen: age 8, height 70 cm, weight 15 kg The question asks for penguins that are "more than 5 years old" and "weigh more than 12 kg". So both conditions must be true. Let's break it down for each penguin. Starting with Louis. His age is 7, which is more than 5. His weight is 11 kg, which is less than 12 kg. So he doesn't meet the second condition. So Louis is out. Next is Bernard. His age is 5. Wait, the condition is "more than 5 years old". So 5 is not more than 5. So Bernard's age is exactly 5, which doesn't satisfy the first condition. Even though his weight is 13 kg (which is more than 12 kg), since his age isn't over 5, he doesn't count. Then Vincent. Age is 9, which is more than 5. His weight is 11 kg, which is less than 12. So again, doesn't meet the weight requirement. So Vincent is out. Lastly, Gwen. Age is 8, which is more than 5. Her weight is 15 kg, which is more than 12. So Gwen meets both conditions. That's one penguin. Wait, let me check again. Are there any others? Let me go through each again to make sure I didn't miss anyone. Louis: age 7 >5, weight 11 <=12 → no Bernard: age 5 not >5 → no Vincent: age 9 >5, weight 11 <=12 → no Gwen: age 8 >5, weight 15 >12 → yes So only Gwen meets both conditions. Therefore, the answer should be 1, which is option A. But let me double-check to be sure. Maybe I made a mistake in the conditions?
